data.title: Maikhan 75MW Pumped Storage Hydro Power Station
data.description: Large-scale renewable energy project featuring a 75MW pumped storage hydroelectric facility serving Mongolia's growing energy needs.
data.content: Project Maikhan is a phased green energy and digital infrastructure program near Ulaanbaatar. Phase 1 delivers a fully permitted pumped storage hydropower (PSH) asset at 75MW (3 x 25MW), with a defined pathway to 100MW through design and procurement optimization. The project includes a 220kV grid connection and approved commercial framework (tariff and PPA), enabling dispatchable low-carbon power for grid balancing. Future phases integrate wind, solar and BESS, then expand PSH capacity to support long-term industrial demand and a green data center roadmap.
data.client: Sunhydro LLC (Project Developer), represented by Monworld Ltd

image.alternativeText: Maikhan Pumped Storage Hydro Power Station

outcomes: 75MW dispatchable PSH capacity ready for phased expansion
Approved permitting package and grid connection framework
Improved peak balancing and ancillary service capability
Foundation for hybrid renewables and green digital infrastructure
challenges: Reservoir and hydraulic optimization across phased scale-up
Detailed design integration with 220kV transmission delivery
Complex EPC packaging and financing close requirements
Maintaining construction schedule under regional climate conditions
data.sourceFile: public/files/PSH 75MW-100MW, Phase1-4, Project Maikhan Brief Intro.docx

data.title: Uvur Lithium and Rubidium Project
data.description: Critical minerals project in Tuv province targeting lithium and rubidium production with advanced geological and metallurgical programs.
data.content: The Uvur Project is located in Tuv province, approximately 75 km east of Ulaanbaatar and around 28 km from Erdene soum. The current program includes detailed geological mapping, geophysical campaigns, trenching, and 10,000 linear meters of drilling. Resource work has been prepared with JORC-aligned methodology and independent technical support. Metallurgical testwork indicates strong recoveries in conceptual process routes for lithium and rubidium-bearing ore. The development path covers conversion from exploration to mining license, feasibility and mine planning, environmental approvals, and staged plant construction toward production.

image.alternativeText: Uvur lithium and rubidium exploration and development

outcomes: Exploration and drilling programs completed for current target areas
JORC-aligned resource estimation workflow established
Conceptual economics indicate strong project potential
Metallurgical testwork demonstrated high lithium and rubidium recovery
challenges: Converting exploration potential into mineable reserves
Scale-up from pilot to commercial processing

data.title: Erdene Power Project
data.description: Energy project profile prepared for investor communication under the EP briefing package.
data.content: This project profile is based on the EP introduction package and is positioned as an early-stage energy infrastructure opportunity in Mongolia. The brief is focused on investment communication and implementation framing, including project rationale, development sequencing, and execution readiness. Scope and final technical parameters are being finalized through ongoing advisory and partner engagement.
outcomes: Investor briefing deck prepared
Project narrative and baseline scope prepared
Initial partner outreach package assembled
Early implementation roadmap drafted
challenges: Aligning delivery model with investor expectations

data.title: Advance Credit Diaspora Fintech Platform
data.description: Execution-ready AU-MN diaspora fintech platform with live remittance rails, verified compliance, and a phased expansion into leasing and deposit products.
data.content: Advance Credit is a Mongolia-Australia fintech platform purpose-built for an underserved diaspora corridor where transfer costs, FX opacity, and compliance friction remain high. The product is already live on both major app stores, with AUSTRAC registration in place and early post-launch transactions completed to validate end-to-end payment flow. The strategic model is phased and capital-efficient: remittance drives customer acquisition and trust; asset-backed apartment leasing monetizes with stronger unit economics through an existing Mongolian underwriting engine; and deposit-wallet products are introduced after licensing progression to lower cost of funds and improve lending scalability. Governance and execution risk are reduced by an established NBFI foundation (operational since 2015), active board oversight, and audited credit operations that provide real balance-sheet support versus typical pre-revenue fintech startups. The current fundraise structure combines growth equity and a senior secured warehouse line to scale originations while limiting unnecessary dilution.
data.value: USD 10M program target (USD 2M equity + USD 8M secured facility)

image.alternativeText: Advance Credit diaspora fintech and remittance platform

outcomes: Remittance application launched on App Store and Google Play (Dec 2025)
AUSTRAC registration completed and AML monitoring workflow active
Initial live transaction set validates cross-border settlement model
Phased expansion plan aligns user acquisition, credit growth, and funding efficiency
Legacy NBFI balance-sheet strength supports scalable underwriting
Compliance tooling and daily monitoring process established with no material findings
Board-led governance model implemented with independent director participation
Fundraise framework prepared: USD 2M equity plus USD 8M senior secured facility
challenges: Refining TAM narrative for corridor-by-corridor expansion
Clarifying equity term language for institutional due diligence
Securing and deploying the USD 8M senior facility at target terms
Managing FX and pricing pressure against larger remittance incumbents
Coordinating multi-jurisdiction compliance while scaling transaction volume
Maintaining strong KYC quality while reducing onboarding friction
Balancing growth pace with credit quality during leasing ramp-up
marketSignals: Mongolian diaspora corridor remains underserved by major providers
Australia-to-Mongolia corridor exhibits niche demand with limited tailored competition
Diaspora customers value local-language support, speed, and transparent FX pricing
